reviews.title: stands up to the big guns of water bottles!!
reviews.text:
What can I say… If Hydro flask had a baby that cost half the price, this would be it!! I’m loving the style and look as, their aesthetically pleasing- BUT, this hits where it counts in performance.. and I’m loving that most of all!!!

Comparing the material of Elvira next to a Hydro flask it goes toe-to-toe both, in all ways. Yess- I’m using Hydro as the measuring stick only because, it’s what all water bottles apparently aspire to be! Personally, in our house- we find water bottles can be left behind far too easy.. or, dropped/dented (compromising their thermal abilities..) so, I prefer to be smart and look for good quality at half the $- at least for The chaotic events where these vanish!!
Loving the fact this has a wide mouth. For any who aren’t certain why this matters… it’s the ability to put ice into the bottle without chiseling a cube piece by piece to fit in a small opening!

This comes with a twist top opening that stays attached and I love it!! Yes, it takes two hands to maneuver but, you don’t run the risk of losing the cap! (and it doesn’t have to be completely removed to drink) Also note, with the standard wide mouth- I’ve tried 3 variations of lids in same size and all fit 100% tight on top of Elvira water bottle. This is huge… Some find a lid that they prefer most but, for various reasons must swap out the bottle- good news with this.. So far, all other widemouth lids seem to fit just fine?! (at least the 3 variations I’ve tried. IF trading lids.. I just suggest, tipping upside-down before doing so, giving peace of mind it wont leak.. I left mine upside down over 1hr. with zero drip.. So, good news all around!)

Personally, I don’t use hot drinks in a water bottle but, I can tell you- after filling 3/4 ice and the remaining space with water. 24hr. later- I still have ice 1/4+ bottle of ice!! This time frame surpasses what description says it will do! I expect the same performance with hot drinks- it should live up to its expectations just fine.
Wide mouth is also easier to clean- although, some bottles state dishwasher safe.. i’ve heard this can cut into bottles “life expectancy” to function properly and with the ease of cleaning, I choose to steer clear of dishwasher- your choice!

Overall- For under $25 I chose a 32oz. water bottle that surpassed expectations at much better price point then, “the big guns!” With fashion forward hombre colors, switchable lid capability and the awesome lid included- This is one I can definitely recommend. If cared for properly it should last as expected.

2 other things FYI’s I want to share:

1. You can actually by water bottle ice trays- they look like a shape/size of a roll of coins and when frozen, they pop out and slide into water bottle with ease..This is a new discovery for us and as avid h2o bottle users- for a few $, it was a great investment. Pop a few into bottle and you’re good to go- super easy!! (you just have to remember to fill the ice tray each night!)

2. If using with any sugary or, flavored substance.. one should empty and rinse out bottle once done. Do not let it sit dirty in sink for a day or 2.. I’ve been told it compromises the integrity and taints the inside of the bottle- ever messing up the flavor moving forward. I also heard never to use with alcohol but… Haven't used in that realm- so, i’m sure if flavor won’t dissipate afterwards?

I hope this info can help in your decision… Feel free to let me know!

reviews.title: Excellent Water Bottle All Around
reviews.text:
Wow. This water bottle kicks butt. Let me tell you why.

(1) First and foremost, it keeps drinks cool (or warm) for HOURS, even when it's in a warm (or cool) environment. I put 6 ice cubes in there and filled it up to the top with water. And my water stayed icy cold for, like, 24 hours. Can you believe it?! Neither can I.
(2) It comes with 3 different lids. So, depending how I'm feeling a particular week or depending what I'm doing (hiking vs camping vs sitting at home, etc), I can easily change out the lid to whatever I feel like.
(3) The bottle itself has a decently wide lip. So, you can clean it fairly well with your hand (if you have small hands & wrists) or with a long scrub brush.
(4) It comes with a little straw scrub brush. To me, this is super important because cleaning little straws would otherwise be impossible and a pain in the butt.
(5) At least one of the lids has a handle that I can hook a belayers hook onto. Very nice for, you know, when I'm doing adventurous stuff like kayaking and hiking and what not.
(6) It is durable. Again, when I'm doing adventurous stuff (or even when I'm sitting at home, let's be honest) it is always good to have a durable water bottle.
(7) The colors look awesome. I got the light blue fades to darker blue color.
(8) It carries a decent amount of liquid. Though it doesn't fit in a cup holder, I need a bigger water bottle like this to force myself to drink more water (cuz it's good for you!).

All in all, I love this water bottle and give it 5/5 stars.

reviews.title: Good Insulation and Comfortable to Hold
reviews.text:
On my bike rides, I was alternating between a single walled plastic water bottle and a metal bottle when I wanted a little more insulation. I wanted something to keep my water cold and I didn't like how my metal water bottle was rattling on my bike.

This bottle is well insulated. It is not going to keep your water as cold as a metal water bottle, but the bottle fits much better in the bike. This is a great alternative to a metal bottle since it keeps the water colder than your typical plastic water bottle. reviews.title: gun metal gray w/ pearlescent shimmer is sweet looking, good seal on lid, NO LEAKS
reviews.text:
I have this in Slate Gray. It is a great look for those who enjoy some shimmer. Classy, upscale vibe to it. I think the dark gun metal gray looks really cool with the pearlescent look.

Good seal with the lid. NO LEAKS. Absolutely no leaks any time I take a sip.

It's an open lid, but no worries on stuff staying hot or cold. However, I do prefer the slider lid style and I wish this had that. I failed to notice the lid style at purchase so that is on me.

I love this style of cup as my daily drink cup. It isn't likely to fit in a car cup holder, but it does sit on a cup holder usually just fine. I love the handle, the size, and the feel of these style tumblers. I also use the non handled kind (usually marketed as a wine tumber) for same use. I just love the size. The addition of handle is the best.

Couple of tricks for any of these cups for maximizing hot and cold is to warm up the cup with hot water for a few minutes (then dump out) before adding hot beverage. Or for starting off extra cold, place empty cup in fridge or freezer for a few minutes. Then add ice and beverage.

reviews.title: A good product concept, but easy to use incorrectly
reviews.text:
I'm giving this 4 stars because when used correctly, it is actually VERY helpful to have on trips! The problem seems to be that they don't make it clear enough how to use properly, and you CAN'T USE IT like a regular water bottle!
The concept is that rather than wait for gravity to pull water through their filter like a traditional Brita pitcher, you fill the bottle with tap water and your suction UP THROUGH THE STRAW is what causes the filtering to happen. That means you can NOT tilt this bottle back and swig from it like a cup or traditional bottle- it won't work! If you try to, it will leak water out the auxiliary hole (used to relieve the vacuum pressure of sucking water up the straw), or it will simply just be too difficult to get it to come out in that orientation. I saw someone else here mention a "hack" to loosen the bottle cap a little which gets it to flow properly out of the straw, but I believe all they are doing is relieving the pressure that is designed to keep it going through the filter up the straw upon suction. That "hack" would also allow water to leak out.
When used correctly, ie right-side up (imagine drinking it like a milkshake), it DOES seem to work and it means we can go on a trip and not be constantly carrying around water bottles. For that it deserves 4 stars alone. But it is very easy to use incorrectly, and even if you KNOW how it works, you sometimes find yourself using it wrong still out of habit. So... lose a star, Brita needs to design this better, or at least make it more clear how it is supposed to work to avoid all the upset buyers. But the concept is a good one and I intend to use it more.

reviews.title: Now you can have drinkable water anywhere!
reviews.text:
I admit to being a water snob. I grew up with filtered water and really don't like tap water for anything except washing dishes. Several years ago Brita had a plastic bottle with a pop up lid that had the filter. I had several but they didn't keep the water cold! Now they've solved that problem. Water stays cold for hours (I don't know about the 24 because I can gulp one of these down in a few hours). The water tastes "right", it's easy to use, easy to clean and has a nice handle. It also seals tight. I haven't had any leaks, even when it's upside down.

I travel A LOT and you can't take your water through security, so when you get to the other side of TSA, you'll have to stand in line to buy a water. I don't anymore!!! I just take my bottle (I fill it with ice and I haven't had any trouble getting ice through, yet) and just use a fountain on the other side. Of course if you'd prefer to stand in a line and spend $5 for a bottle of drinkable water that will get warm in an hour, this isn't for you. I haven't tested this outside for long periods of time and I won't until summer goes away but I expect it will work just fine. Highly recommended.

reviews.title: so our need for water was not as great as it would be on a hot day in ...
reviews.text:
I purchased four of these vapur water bottles in different colors for a family trip to DisneyWorld. The goal was for each family member to carry their own water (and other basic items) for a full day at the parks. We were there in January, so our need for water was not as great as it would be on a hot day in August. We basically used one entire bottle each per day, plus 1-2 purchased drinks and only on one day, a refill of the vapur at a water fountain. On an average day, the full bottle was enough without a refill. We bought a case of bottled water at an Orlando supermarket for $6 and kept it in our hotel room. Each morning, we filled the vapur bottles. This saved some money and saved us time that might have been spent waiting in line to buy water.

The vapur bottles don't look very sturdy, but we used them for a solid week and didn't have a single spill, tear, or leak. The caps stayed shut even though they were jostled around in backpacks for hours on end. The carabiner clip can be used to wear the bottle, but we just threw ours in our small backpacks. When the bottles are empty, they can be rolled up and tucked into the clip, taking up less space.

There was no problem taking the empty bottles through airport security.

I am very happy with this item and feel it was a smart purchase.

reviews.title: Some pros, some cons
reviews.text:
First the pros:
*The water inside it tastes like nothing which is exactly how you want water to taste.
*It's very clever and portable.
*The lid fits very securely to prevent leaks from the top.

And then the cons:
*You can't put regular ice in it (though you can freeze the whole thing ahead of a hike or whatever).
*It's very difficult to fill without spilling.
*The seams are not very secure making me fear leaks are in the not too distant future.

I'm really on the fence about this bottle. The idea is super cool and it has a lot of potential. The execution is just not that great, however. I'll put it in my emergency bag because, really, that's the only scenario I can imagine actually wanting to use it in.

reviews.title: Finally something that helps me drink more water!!!
reviews.text:
I love this infuser bottle! I purchased this because I’m trying to drink more water. Anyone that knows me, I am a Mountain Dew drinker and I needed something to help me to drink actual water. This year I am trying to work on my health more because I have been recently diagnosed as a diabetic and I also have Hypothyroidism. Anyway, this infuser is it! I purchased two at first, so I can drink one and the other is in the fridge to soak up the lemons and to stay cold. I wish I knew about these a long time ago. I’ve tried for years to drink water more. I’ve tried using crystal light and drops to put in your water, and I’ve tried flavored water, but I was always left with a dry taste in my mouth. When I’ve told other people this, they would think I was just weird. But I honestly have avoided drinking water because it would just make me feel even more thirsty. After trying this for the first time, I used lemons and put them in the infuser. I am now a huge fan of lemon water!!! I can now drink water without having that after taste and I finally feel like I’m refreshed after drinking water.

This infuser is made of hard plastic and comes with a time schedule written on the outside of the bottle so that you can keep track of your water intake as well and to make sure that you drink plenty of water throughout the day. There is also a sleeve you can put the plastic bottle in to help keep the water nice and cold throughout your day. You can tell it looks like it is quality made. I have enjoyed this infuser so much that I just recently purchased two more, but in a different color. Now I can have three cold bottles in the fridge, while carrying one around with me. I leave the others in the fridge for a quick drink when I’m thirsty as opposed to just grabbing a Mountain Dew. At the end of the day I wash them out and put new lemons in for the next day to soak in overnight. This infuser also comes with a little recipe book as to which fruits you can combine and how it promotes your health. I have yet to try anything other than lemons so far, because I just can’t seem to get enough of the lemon water. Soon I will be trying out other fruits that are suggested just so that I don’t get tired of the lemons. There is also a locking mechanism on the cap so that nothing spills from the bottle. My bottles have fallen on their side and so far nothing has come out to make a mess. I’ve been using this now for a few months and they still work as perfect as the first day I received them.

For those of you that are like me that avoid drinking water like it’s the plague, I highly recommend purchasing this infuser. I just love the fact that I can drink water again! Thank you so much for this!!! This infuser is definitely a life changer for me!!!

reviews.title: Arrived Damaged!!

The space is too big so you have to cut your fruit small enough to fit inside of the tube but big enough that it won't slip through the top and release chunks into your mouth through the gap while you are drinking the water. That is very irritating but I told myself I could deal with it. The leaking on the other hand irritates me to the point that it has ruined this bottle/experience for me. I finally figured out where the leak was coming from and the part that is supposed to keep the bottle sealed once it is closed is messed up. It looks like it was melted. I have washed this bottle by hand since I have had it. So it was an error on the producer's part. Of course my return window has closed by just a few days so I am now stuck with a bottle that doesn't work. Save your money and or don't spend it on this, you'd be better off purchasing a different bottle.

I purchased this to replace a different brand of bottle that got terminal black mold, no matter how much I washed it. I couldn't replace the exact bottle, but this seems like a close fit. I like the lock mechanism, and the flip top button, which my old bottle had. I also didn't want one with a straw. I am a little worried that some buyers say that the lock breaks after a while, and I'm hoping mine doesn't. I guess if it does, I will simply look for another version of the lock-type, chug-type drinking bottle. The 4 stars are because of the deceptive photo of two bottles, and the initial description that talks about "bottles". Otherwise it would be a five star rating.

reviews.title: 2 reviews in 1: Copper mugs keeps it hot hot, stainless steel keeps it warm & drinkable
reviews.text:
This is a review for the 14 ounce COPPER mug, and the 12 ounce STAINLESS STEEL mug.

Update Aug 2022: My review below (from 2019) is for the short squat silver mugs that I bought back in 2019. I recently purchased the COPPER colored geometric shape cup as part of a "copper" themed 7th wedding anniversary gift for my husband (Aug 2022). SO I will add out experience with that mug here. Also grabbed some [[ASIN:B01L9YVAAY Copper Moon Keurig coffee]] to go with it.

He texted me from work, "copper mug works, but omg it holds heat. Have to transfer to my ceramic mug to drink in a timely fashion. Who the hell wants scalding hot coffee an hour later?" I respond, "most people"

Gave me a chuckle, and thought I would add it to the review. He likes his coffee drinkable. Not necessarily cool, but not hot hot. The silver mugs (below) will lose heat, but it holds coffee very well, and keeps it warm enough to be drinkable. This copper mug will keep it hot hot for some time. Perfect for those that want hot coffee for as long as possible. Seems very secure, air tight. THE GRIP IS GREAT. Cool looking too. LOVE the look of the copper color. Has a.. copper appearance. Not a flat color. Fits under my Keurig if I pop out the tray. Smaller mug reviewed below fits underneath without any adjustment.

------------------------------------------------------------------
Original review for STAINLESS STEEL 12 ounce mug written Feb 2019: I bought two, one each for my husband and me. It is a 12oz travel mug with a silicone band for grip and protection from the hot. The base has piece of grip material, that will help it from sliding and also from making loud bang noise when being set down (say on a table during a conference call where all sounds are amplified). The lid is sturdy and quite secure. I filled the cup, closed it up, then tipped it upside down. No leaks! The contents does not stay super hot for very long. I didn't time it, but it kept the contents warm enough and long enough to have a nice cup of coffee/tea. Because it is 12oz, you have to figure out which brewing level will work best for you. Just black? make 12oz if your Keurig has that configuration (or two 6oz). Like cream and sugar? Go for 10oz so you have room to add the other items. I like that it isn't a huge cup, though I also like using the larger ones. It is just a handy little size, and feels good in the hand. I haven't had it long enough to determine it's durability. I suspect that the bottom grip material could pull off if the cup sits in water. It isn't made for long term heat retention, so consider that as well. Overall good cup, just not a perfect cup.

Update Feb 2020: Came back nearly a year later to say that our two Keurig travel mugs are still holding up, and that they are now my preferred travel mug. I am currently getting rid of any other travel mug and buying two more of these.

reviews.title: I like them a lot
reviews.text:
I liked the first one so much, I bought 2 more. I got one for my cold drinks, one for the hot drinks and one for my mother.

Breakdown:
So many people complained about chipping, I've had mine for a month or more and just hand wash. I also don't let it sit in the sink to be scratched by other objects. I've not seen any chipping.

When I fill this with large grocery store ice cubes, the ice lasts for 2 days when the lid is closed and only opened to drink. Maybe 1.5 days when the lid is open. Chipped ice lasts maybe 1/2 a day (a little longer when the lid is closed.

My hot drinks have decent warm time for me. I drink my hots straight from the bottle vs pouring it to a container. I'd say, I have to wait about 4-6 hours to drink not scalding hot drinks. If you're looking for day long scalding hot, this ain't for you.

Though this does not typically leak. I have had some leaks. User error. When I fill this up to the brim with a very hot beverage & close the lid, chemistry ends up happening and hot air is gonna find a way to escape while spitting fluids out. Use the line with your hots. 🤷🏾‍♀️

I've literally used both mugs every day that I've had them. I start off the day with my colds and end the day with my hots. They work for me.

Mom likes hers fine but feels it's too big. She wants the benefit of the 32 ounces but found the diameter a bit cumbersome. This will not fit under your coffee machine in a stable fashion but the lean method works. 😩😄

I'd recommend. Oh, and the 3 colors I've purchased were true to the pic: the deep purple to blue, the dark blue to a lighter blue and the pink to teal. I'm not fitna look those names up for the review though... 😄
